text: ECONOMICS: Brent $106.07 (-$2 from Day 75 PM close) on diplomacy optimism from Trump-Xi summit. WTI $101.54 essentially flat. Gas $4.511 unchanged. Wheat $6.70/bu (pulling back from limit-up $6.79 after USDA crop rating 28% good-to-excellent — worst since 2022). Pentagon confirms US war cost now $29B — up from $25B end of April. Hormuz Day 73. Ceasefire expires May 17.
verificationNote: OilPrice.com and TradingEconomics confirmed Brent/WTI data. AAA confirmed gas price. TradingEconomics confirmed wheat. Navy Times confirmed Pentagon $29B figure.

text: NETANYAHU CBS 60 MINUTES: 'War is not over until highly enriched uranium is removed — nuclear facilities must be dismantled.' On regime change: 'possible, not guaranteed.' Earlier: Jerusalem Day speech at Ammunition Hill — 'regime of terror in Iran is weaker than ever, State of Israel is stronger than ever.' Two statements in one day signal Netanyahu pivoting to a hardened post-ceasefire posture.
verificationNote: CBS News confirmed 60 Minutes interview quotes. Times of Israel liveblog confirmed Jerusalem Day speech. Iran International confirmed regime change quote.

text: SHIPPING INCIDENT: Indian-flagged cargo ship 'Haji Ali' attacked and sunk off Oman coast while en route Somalia→Sharjah. All 14 Indian crew rescued by Oman Coast Guard. No party claimed responsibility. 2nd major shipping incident today after Fujairah tanker seizure. Pattern consistent with IRGC retaliatory ops vs non-Iranian-flagged vessels.
verificationNote: Washington Times confirmed sinking and crew rescue. Al Jazeera confirmed Oman coast guard rescue. Haaretz confirmed no claim of responsibility.

text: IDF SOUTH LEBANON: 65 Hezbollah sites struck in 24 hours — 20+ operatives killed. Weapon depots, surveillance posts, command nodes. 1 killed in Sidon car airstrike. IDF issues evacuation orders for 6 south Lebanon villages. All this on the day Lebanon-Israel ceasefire Round 3 talks open in DC. Hezbollah drone also hit Israeli territory at Rosh Hanikra. Tension rising ahead of May 17 ceasefire deadline.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed 65 sites and 20+ killed. Al Jazeera confirmed Sidon strike. NPR confirmed DC peace talks.

text: HOUTHIS NOT DONE: IDF intercepts Houthi drone near Eilat — first attack on city since April 8 Iran-US ceasefire. No sirens; drone downed before reaching populated areas. US CENTCOM separately destroys 4 Houthi UAS in Yemen. Houthis continue operating independently of Iran ceasefire — Red Sea shipping threat unresolved. Third front remains active.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed Eilat drone interception as first since ceasefire. CENTCOM statement confirmed 4 Houthi UAS destroyed in Yemen.

text: INTELLIGENCE GAP: NYT/WaPo intelligence reports directly contradict Trump's claims — Iran retained ~70% of pre-war missile arsenal, restored access to ~30 of 33 missile sites, 90% of underground facilities 'partially or fully operational.' Trump on Beijing trip claimed 'Iran's entire Navy at bottom of sea.' Hegseth told Congress Iran's missile program is 'functionally destroyed.' Both claims false per US intel.
verificationNote: WaPo confirmed CIA/DIA intelligence assessments showing 70% retention. NYT independently confirmed 30 of 33 sites restored. CNN analysis confirmed direct contradiction of public statements. Congressional Democrats confirmed accessing same classified briefings.

text: HORMUZ SLIGHT EASING: Bloomberg/gCaptain report ~4 supertankers exited Hormuz since May 10 carrying ~2M bbl/day of Iraqi crude — oil flows 'creeping higher.' Still far below pre-war ~20M bbl/day. 30 China-linked vessels transited under Iran permit protocols during Trump-Xi summit. Analysts: 'so low it won't make much of a difference.' IRGC fast-boat swarms still active in strait. Brent $105.87, WTI $101.42, Gas $4.534.
verificationNote: Bloomberg and gCaptain confirmed supertanker exits. Al Jazeera confirmed 30 China-linked vessels. Fox News reported IRGC fast-boat swarms. FX Leaders and OilPriceAPI confirmed Brent/WTI prices. AAA confirmed gas price.

text: IRAN AT BRICS: Araghchi in New Delhi calls BRICS 'a new world order' and says 'declining imperialist powers are reacting with desperation.' Specifically warns UAE that countries 'collaborating with Israel will be held accountable.' His plane bore inscription 'Minab168' — honoring 168 children killed in US strikes on Feb 28. First high-level Iranian multilateral diplomacy since war began — signals Tehran seeking non-Western bloc support.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and WION confirmed Araghchi BRICS speech and direct quotes. Plane inscription confirmed by Iranian state media IRIB.

text: IRGC POSTURE: Brigadier-General Mohammad Akrami Nia declares Iran military at 'the highest level of readiness — there is no room for retreat.' Statement signals Tehran's hardline posture even as Araghchi engages diplomatically in New Delhi and nuclear talks continue. Pattern consistent with Iran's dual-track strategy: diplomacy for time, military preparation for escalation.
verificationNote: Iran Monitor and Tasnim News confirmed Akrami Nia statement. Context verified by IRGC's 'Martyr Commander' exercise confirmation by Tasnim.

text: HORMUZ VLCC NAMED: Chinese supertanker Yuan Hua Hu — carrying approximately 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude — confirmed transited Strait of Hormuz during Trump-Xi summit window. First named major Chinese vessel passage since Iran launched PGSA permit protocols. Indicates Iran selectively opening transit for strategic partners while keeping strait 'effectively closed' to others.
verificationNote: Bloomberg and gCaptain confirmed VLCC Yuan Hua Hu transit. Al Jazeera confirmed Chinese vessel transit during summit. PGSA protocol context confirmed by multiple shipping sources.

text: NETANYAHU-UAE CONTROVERSY: Netanyahu's office announced secret visit to UAE's Mohammed bin Zayed during war — UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs flatly denied it, calling report 'entirely unfounded.' Separately, Iran FM Araghchi warned UAE 'will be held accountable' for Israel collaboration. US Ambassador Huckabee confirmed Israel sent Iron Dome batteries and operators to UAE. Three conflicting signals on same day regarding UAE-Israel relationship.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed Netanyahu office announcement. UAE MFA denial confirmed by Reuters and Al Arabiya. Araghchi UAE warning confirmed by Al Jazeera. Huckabee Iron Dome confirmation from Times of Israel.

text: CONGRESS VS TRUMP: Bipartisan group of 52 senators + 177 House members demand Trump reject any Iran deal allowing enrichment. Led by Gottheimer (D) + Bacon (R) — rare cross-aisle agreement. Same week Hegseth told Congress Trump needs no approval to restart strikes, and Caine refused to confirm classified BDA in public hearing. Congress asserting war oversight amid 60-day War Powers clock. Three separate congressional actions on Iran in one day.
verificationNote: Gottheimer press release confirmed letter. CNN confirmed Senate BDA hearing. WaPo confirmed bipartisan frustration in Hegseth/Caine hearing.

text: TRUMP-XI SUMMIT: Joint statement confirms Hormuz 'must remain open' and Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon. Xi offered Iran mediation, pledged no Chinese military equipment to Iran. Rubio clarified: 'US did not ask China for help on Iran — this was China's offer.' Analysts note China simultaneously allowing its vessels through Hormuz under Iranian permit protocols — selective transit raising questions about neutrality.
verificationNote: NBC News confirmed Rubio post-summit statement. Time confirmed Trump-Xi details. CNBC confirmed joint statement elements.

text: IRAN NAVAL ACTION: Honduras-flagged Hui Chuan vessel seized 38nm NE of Fujairah by IRGC, taken toward Iranian waters (UKMTO confirmed). Vessel was operating as floating armory. Separately, Indian cargo ship Haji Ali sunk off Oman — 14 crew rescued by Omani coast guard. Both incidents occurred simultaneously with Trump-Xi summit. Iran VP Aref: 'We will not give up Hormuz at any cost.'
verificationNote: Euronews confirmed Hui Chuan seizure via UKMTO. Bloomberg confirmed. Washington Times confirmed Haji Ali sinking.

text: LEBANON DAY 77: IDF struck 65+ Hezbollah sites in south Lebanon over 24h — 20+ operatives killed. Separately, Hezbollah launched rockets at N. Israel + fiber-optic drone wounded 3 IDF soldiers. IDF declared ceasefire breach. Lebanon-Israel peace talks (Round 3) in Washington as ceasefire expires May 18. Talks expected to produce extension without full halt to IDF strikes. Lebanon death toll since March 2: 2,898 confirmed.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed Hezbollah ceasefire breach. IDF confirmed 65+ sites struck. NPR confirmed Round 3 talks in Washington.

text: IRAN NUCLEAR TALKS DEADLOCK: Iran has set 5 preconditions for resuming formal negotiations: (1) all sanctions lifted; (2) war reparations paid; (3) US naval blockade on Iranian ports ended; (4) frozen assets unfrozen; (5) Iranian management of Strait of Hormuz recognized. Trump rejected Iran's counter-proposal to US 14-point plan as 'totally unacceptable.' Ceasefire described as 'on massive life support.' Pakistan mediating.
verificationNote: NBC News confirmed Iran's 5 preconditions. Al Jazeera confirmed Trump rejection of Iranian counter-proposal. CBS News confirmed ceasefire characterization.

text: HEGSETH CONGRESS: Defense Secretary testified before Congress amid bipartisan grilling over $29B Iran war cost (up $4B from prior estimate) and shrinking munitions stockpile. Called concerns 'foolishly and unhelpfully overstated.' Pentagon now seeking additional supplemental funding. CIA/DIA assessments contradict public claims — Iran retained ~70% of pre-war missile arsenal, 90% of underground facilities operational.
verificationNote: WaPo and PBS confirmed Hegseth congressional testimony. Defense News confirmed $29B figure. Breaking Defense confirmed not including base damage costs. CNN confirmed intelligence assessment discrepancy.

displayName: ISW (Institute for the Study of War)
text: ISW ASSESSMENT — Day 77: Lebanon ceasefire (expiring May 18) will likely be extended but Israel will not halt south Lebanon operations. Iran rejected US 14-point MOU demanding 20-year enrichment freeze + surrender of 440kg 60%-enriched uranium. Iran's counter: 5-year freeze, retain enrichment. Mojtaba Khamenei has not made verified public appearance since March 30 airstrike. Netanyahu's claimed UAE secret visit publicly denied by Abu Dhabi.
verificationNote: ISW daily Iran update. MadaMasr confirmed ceasefire extension without strike halt. WSJ confirmed Mojtaba Khamenei absence. Times of Israel confirmed Netanyahu UAE revelation and UAE denial.

text: HEZBOLLAH CEASEFIRE BREAKDOWN: IDF reports Hezbollah mortar fire killed Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan (20, Golani Brigade) in south Lebanon — 6th IDF death since ceasefire, 19th since war escalation. IDF intercepted Hezbollah rocket targeting Lower Galilee (sirens in Masad/Eilabun). IDF issued urgent evacuation warning for 8 villages near Tyre ahead of retaliatory strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog May 15 confirmed all three incidents — IDF soldier death, rocket interception, and evacuation warnings for Tyre-area villages.

text: TRUMP-XI SUMMIT DAY 2: Key outcomes — (1) Xi pledged no military equipment to Iran; (2) Both agreed Strait of Hormuz cannot be militarized or tolled; (3) China confirmed it will continue buying Iranian oil regardless; (4) US and China agreed Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon. Rubio: 'He didn't ask them for anything.' Xi offered to help broker peace — but Foreign Policy notes China summit produced 'few wins' for Trump on concrete Iran commitments.
verificationNote: Time, CBS News, NBC News, CNBC, and Al Jazeera confirmed all four Trump-Xi summit outcomes. Foreign Policy analysis confirmed limited concrete wins.

text: BRICS NEW DELHI UPDATE: Iran FM Araghchi addressed Day 2 of BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting (May 14-15), calling on members to 'unequivocally condemn' US-Israeli 'illegal aggression.' Said Iranians 'never bow to any pressure' and accused UAE of direct military involvement. BRICS failed to reach unified condemnation — UAE pushed back. Russia's Lavrov, Brazil, South Africa attended; China sent ambassador instead of Wang Yi.
verificationNote: PressTV, Al Jazeera, The Tribune India, ABC News, and Modern Diplomacy confirmed Araghchi's statements and the BRICS internal division over Iran-UAE dispute.

text: NETANYAHU JERUSALEM DAY (May 15): Netanyahu declared at Ammunition Hill ceremony: 'Iran is weaker than ever, Israel is stronger than ever.' Claimed Israeli preemptive strikes prevented Iran from obtaining a nuclear bomb. Also announced (with FM Sa'ar) that Israel will sue the New York Times over Nick Kristof column alleging systematic sexual abuse of Palestinian detainees. Israel calls the piece 'one of the most hideous and distorted lies ever published.'
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog May 15 confirmed Netanyahu Jerusalem Day speech and the NYT lawsuit announcement. Both quotes attributed to Netanyahu at the Ammunition Hill ceremony.

text: DIPLOMATIC ROUNDUP DAY 77: (1) Ceasefire technically holds but Lebanon front is active — IDF striking near Tyre after Hezbollah mortar/rocket attacks; (2) Pakistan mediating US-Iran talks — ceasefire expires May 17; (3) Iran at BRICS pushing non-Western coalition condemnation of US/Israel; (4) Pezeshkian praised 'Third Imposed War' resistance in national unity video; (5) Iran VP Aref: 'We will not give up the Strait of Hormuz at any cost. The matter is closed.'
verificationNote: Al Jazeera Day 77 liveblog, PressTV (Aref quote), Times of Israel (Lebanon front), and PressTV (Pezeshkian video) confirm all five items.

text: TRUMP POLICY SHIFT DAY 77: Departing Beijing Air Force One, Trump signals he's now open to 20-YEAR Iranian nuclear suspension (previously demanded permanent ban) 'if it's real.' Also weighing lifting sanctions on Chinese companies buying Iranian oil — decision 'in next few days.' Al Jazeera analysis: Xi-Trump summit yielded no binding commitments; Euronews: 'underwhelming outcome brings Trump back to reality.' Iran ceasefire expires May 17.
verificationNote: Trump Air Force One remarks confirmed by CNBC and Times of Israel. Al Jazeera and Euronews confirmed summit analysis. No binding Iran agreement from Trump-Xi meeting.

text: ARAGHCHI URANIUM DEADLOCK (Day 77 AM): Iranian FM confirms US-Iran nuclear talks in 'deadlock' over enriched uranium — issue 'postponed, not under discussion.' Iran has 'no trust' in US; 'contradictory messages from Washington' make Tehran doubt seriousness. Russia's offer to store enriched uranium 'appreciated but not on agenda.' Araghchi met India FM Jaishankar bilaterally at BRICS New Delhi — Iran diversifying diplomatic options.
verificationNote: Araghchi uranium deadlock and 'no trust' quote confirmed by WION and Al Jazeera. Jaishankar bilateral meeting confirmed by Republic World and Times of Israel.

displayName: ISW (Institute for the Study of War)
text: IRGC 'MARTYRED LEADER' EXERCISE: IRGC Mohammad Rasulullah Division launched 5-day war game near Tehran (began May 12-13) simulating US GROUND INVASION — RPGs, mortars, snipers, armed drones, motorcycle assault tactics. Gen. Akrami Nia: 'highest level of readiness, no room for retreat.' Context: CIA assessed Iran can withstand blockade for months, has 70% of pre-war missiles. This exercise is not symbolic — it's preparing for resumed warfare.
verificationNote: IRGC exercise confirmed by Tasnim News Agency and Israel Hayom. CIA/DIA missile assessment confirmed by Middle East Eye, i24NEWS, and Irish Times. ISW assessment context.

text: KHAMENEI ABSENCE: Mojtaba Khamenei has NOT made a verified public appearance in 60+ days since March 30 airstrike killed his father, wife, and son. Iran claims 'just a fracture.' Independent reports say severe burns, possible incapacitation. AI-generated images and ghostwritten statements substituting for real appearances. US intel (CNN, May 8): 'mentally sharp but isolated, ruling through tight inner circle.' Iran running on autopilot.
verificationNote: Khamenei absence and AI images confirmed by The National, Voice of Emirates, and WSJ. CNN confirmed US intelligence assessment of 'mentally sharp but isolated.' Iran officially denies serious injury.

text: ECONOMICS DAY 77 AM: Brent crude $106.89/bbl (+33.9% from pre-war), WTI $101.17/bbl (+34.0%). Slight morning pullback from Day 76 close ($107.30/$102.74). IEA warns global oil market will remain 'materially undersupplied' through OCTOBER even if conflict resolves next month. OPEC+ June increase: +188K bpd (symbolic — can't compensate for Hormuz closure). Gas: $4.534/gal. GasBuddy: $5/gal by Memorial Day if Hormuz stays closed.
verificationNote: Brent/WTI prices confirmed by TradingEconomics and CNBC. IEA undersupply warning confirmed by IEA Oil Market Report. OPEC+ increase confirmed by Al Jazeera. AAA confirmed gas price. GasBuddy forecast confirmed.

text: 12TH ROUND ECONOMIC FURY SANCTIONS (May 11): OFAC designated 12 individuals/entities for enabling IRGC oil sales to China. Separately, US sanctioned Chinese petroleum terminal Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal Co. for importing tens of millions of barrels of Iranian crude. EU separately sanctioned 19 Iranian officials (May 6). Economic Fury = US campaign to cut off Iran's oil revenue while blockade forces Hormuz closure. China continues buying regardless.
verificationNote: OFAC 12th round confirmed by Treasury press release (home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0498). Qingdao terminal sanctions confirmed by State Department. EU sanctions confirmed by Reuters.
